scope_contract.tagging_rule: every finding includes introduced_in_diff: true|false per AC-12.1 Mechanism (ii)

docs_categorization.reason: Auto-generated README inventory is stale on first generation — declares 'Total files: 1' and lists only test_AC_01 while sibling INDEX.md (same timestamp) enumerates all 4 entries (manifest.json + 3 test files). Inventory drift is new-in-this-diff because the file itself is new in this cycle.
docs_categorization.ownership: doc-sync hook (hooks/doc_sync/regen_readme.py) — content is auto-generated by the <!-- AUTO:readme-stats --> template, not authored by dev
docs_categorization.recommendation_rationale: Regenerating the AUTO block from inside this cycle is the doc-sync hook's job; dev cannot manually edit the AUTO block without violating the hook's ownership contract. The drift is an artifact of when the hook last ran relative to file creation timestamps, not a dev cleanliness failure. Surface as advisory so close.md may decide whether to invoke the hook re-run before the cycle is finalized.

verdict_rationale: No new-in-this-diff cleanliness violations attributable to dev's intentional changes. Hook file (+141/-32) is a pure functional change with no debug prints / commented-out code / TODO-FIXME / temp markers; stable stderr token preserved. Three new test files follow standard naming/structure (test_AC_04_bypass_20260525.py is explicitly permitted by manifest.json L73-75 sibling-file convention). __pycache__/ is gitignored. The one minor docs_categorization finding (auto-generated README stale on first generation) is advisory_only because its content is owned by the doc-sync hook template, not by dev edits — per AC-2.6 default-safe rule for file-level findings without dev-actionable scope.

ac_2_6_plumbing.rationale: Per AC-2.6 (b) NEW-positive marker rule with introduced_in_diff tagging: the single tagged finding is severity=minor with recommendation=advisory_only (hook-owned content), not a blocker. No major/critical findings in scope. CLOSE: YES on cleanliness gate.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex flagged ONE false negative (PROPOSED_FIX provided): tests/generated/20260525-095242/README.md is new-in-this-diff and its folder inventory is stale (says 'Total files: 1' while INDEX.md lists 4). Codex agreed __pycache__/ is correctly excluded (gitignored) and the test_AC_04_bypass_20260525.py sibling-file naming is correctly excluded (manifest-documented convention). One OBSERVATION_ONLY agreeing with two of my exclusion decisions.

findings.issue: tests/generated/20260525-095242/README.md is new-in-this-diff with stale folder inventory (Total files: 1, lists only test_AC_01, while INDEX.md enumerates all 4 entries).
findings.proposed_fix: Regenerate or update README.md so it lists all 4 entries and aligns naming/convention metadata with INDEX.md; do not frame as hook correctness in the cleanliness report.
findings.rationale: Added as docs_categorization finding tagged introduced_in_diff: true, severity=minor, recommendation=advisory_only. Reframed per codex guidance: I do NOT cast this as a hook correctness issue. I DO disagree with codex on actionability — the AUTO block is exclusively written by hooks/doc_sync/regen_readme.py from the <!-- AUTO:readme-stats --> template; dev hand-editing the AUTO block would violate the hook's content ownership contract. So the finding is surfaced as advisory_only, leaving close.md to decide whether a hook re-run inside this cycle is warranted. The finding is recorded with PROPOSED_FIX preserved verbatim for the close orchestrator's reference.

findings.issue: OBSERVATION: __pycache__/ correctly excluded (gitignored); test_AC_04_bypass_20260525.py correctly excluded (manifest-documented sibling-file convention).
findings.rationale: Codex confirmed two of my exclusion decisions. No action required.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Added one docs_categorization finding with introduced_in_diff: true and recommendation=advisory_only, preserving codex's PROPOSED_FIX verbatim. Verdict remains PASS / CLOSE_YES because the finding is minor + advisory, not blocking per AC-2.6 (b).
